evidence_quotes.quote: the seller of the product may have abolished the possibility of using a product already paid by the consumer.

I would therefore like to see a clarification of consumer protection for digital shopping so that the customer has access to the product for as long as the product is reproducible with the available equipment.
store and use that program even after the service provider has left

paragraphs.text: With regard to digital material, there has been a long-standing grey situation where money changes ownership, but ownership of the purchase has been unclear or even blurred. It would be desirable for the digital product to be afforded the same protection as physical products. The problem has been that the seller of the product may have abolished the possibility of using a product already paid by the consumer. In my view, that should not be the case. I would therefore like to see a clarification of consumer protection for digital shopping so that the customer has access to the product for as long as the product is reproducible with the available equipment. For example, music or a video can be reproduced or recorded in the music or film you buy. And in digital games and programmes, that programme may be performed by compatible devices, even if the original digital distribution ceases, either by offering an alternative means, or by the possibility to store and use that program even after the service provider has left, either by means of open code or otherwise to guarantee the functionality of the programme by means of devices that were compatible with the shelf life of the programme.
